title: varronia cremersii
intro: Varronia cremersii, also known as the bush mango or wild mango, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Varronia cremersii include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system and promote wound healing. Varronia cremersii is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture by herbalists, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
